message.content:
## Web Searcher: intelligibility enhancement and silence compression

Research question: "Best algorithms/techniques to make audio the most comprehensible (e.g. time-scale modification like WSOLA/PSOLA, intelligibility enhancement, silence/pause compression, spectral and prosody processing), AND how "speed listening" works at playback speeds greater than 16x — the perceptual limits of accelerated speech comprehension, training/adaptation effects, and the signal-processing methods that make extreme speed-ups intelligible."

Your angle: **intelligibility enhancement and silence compression** — Comprehensibility methods like silence trimming and prosody shaping, distinct from raw time-stretching.
Search query: `speech intelligibility enhancement silence pause removal spectral prosody processing smart speed dynamic compression`

## Task
Use WebSearch with the query above (or a refined version). Return the top 4-6 most relevant results.
Rank by relevance to the ORIGINAL question, not just the search query. Skip obvious SEO spam/content farms.
Include a short snippet capturing why each result is relevant.

Structured output only.

attachment.content:
- caveman: Ultra-compressed communication mode. Cuts token usage ~75% by speaking like caveman while keeping full technical accuracy. Supports intensity levels: lite, full (default), ultra, wenyan-lite, wenyan-full, wenyan-ultra. Use when user says "caveman mode", "talk like caveman", "use caveman", "less tokens", "be brief", or invokes /caveman. Also auto-triggers when token efficiency is requested.
- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- frontend-design:frontend-design: Create distinctive, production-grade frontend interfaces with high design quality. Use this skill when the user asks to build web components, pages, or applications. Generates creative, polished code that avoids generic AI aesthetics.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use when asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, check that a feature works, or validate local changes before pushing.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled cloud agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring cloud agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Fable, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

silence removal audio podcast smart speed algorithm pause compression playback speed comprehension

content.content:
Web search results for query: "speech intelligibility enhancement silence pause removal spectral prosody processing smart speed dynamic compression"

Links: [{"title":"Enhancing Speech Intelligibility in Text-To-Speech Synthesis using   Speaking Style Conversion","url":"https://arxiv.org/pdf/2008.05809"},{"title":"(PDF) Speech-in-noise intelligibility improvement based on spectral shaping and dynamic range compression","url":"https://www.academia.edu/113840667/Speech_in_noise_intelligibility_improvement_based_on_spectral_shaping_and_dynamic_range_compression"},{"title":"Speech intelligibility enhancement","url":"https://image-ppubs.uspto.gov/dirsearch-public/print/downloadPdf/4454609"},{"title":"REAL-TIME SPEECH-IN-NOISE INTELLIGIBILITY ENHANCEMENT BASED ON","url":"https://www.2014.ieeeicassp.org/files/ST/1569914275%20-%20Real-Time%20Speech-in-Noise%20Intelligibility%20Enhancement%20.pdf"},{"title":"Speech processing system for enhancing speech to be outputted in a noisy environment","url":"https://image-ppubs.uspto.gov/dirsearch-public/print/downloadPdf/10636433"},{"title":"Combining spectral and temporal modification techniques for speech intelligibility enhancement - ScienceDirect","url":"https://www.sciencedirect.com/science/article/abs/pii/S0885230818300676"},{"title":"(PDF) Speech-in-noise intelligibility improvement based on spectral shaping and dynamic range compression","url":"https://www.researchgate.net/publication/288412949_Speech-in-noise_intelligibility_improvement_based_on_spectral_shaping_and_dynamic_range_compression"},{"title":"Spectral shaping for speech intelligibility enhancement","url":"https://image-ppubs.uspto.gov/dirsearch-public/print/downloadPdf/9196258"}]

The search results reveal several key approaches to speech intelligibility enhancement:

## Core Techniques

Speech intelligibility in noise can be improved through signal processing techniques such as amplitude compression, changes in spectral tilts, formant sharpening, and dynamic range compression.

## Spectral Shaping and Dynamic Range Compression (SSDRC)

A state-of-the-art method called Spectral Shaping and Dynamic Range Compression (SSDRC) provides high intelligibility gains in various noisy conditions by redistributing signal energy on time-frequency information. This combines spectral shaping to enhance quality in frequency followed by dynamic range compression to enhance quality in time.

## Processing Approach

Many algorithms for speech modification operate by redistributing speech energy across the spectrum, with most algorithms leaving the timing of underlying speech sounds intact. Some techniques alter the timing of speech relative to the masker, and both spectral and temporal approaches lead to a reduction in energetic masking.

## Real-Time Implementation

A speech intelligibility enhancing system can apply a spectral shaping filter and dynamic range compression, with at least one of the control parameters updated in real time according to the speech received.

The search results focus primarily on spectral and dynamic range compression techniques rather than specific mention of pause removal or prosody processing features in this exact combination.


REMINDER: You MUST include the sources above in your response to the user using markdown hyperlinks.

Web search results for query: "silence removal audio podcast smart speed algorithm pause compression playback speed comprehension"

Links: [{"title":"The 1.25x vs 1.5x Showdown: Which Speed is Best for You?","url":"https://audiobookspeedcalc.com/blog/125-vs-150-showdown"},{"title":"Introducing Trim Silence: save seconds by intelligently removing pauses – RadioPublic","url":"https://about.radiopublic.com/2019/03/11/introducing-trim-silence-save-seconds-by-intelligently-removing-pauses/"},{"title":"Remove Silence from Video: Smaller Files & Better Pacing — Klipa AI Blog","url":"https://klipa.ai/en/blog/remove-silence-from-video_474"},{"title":"Remove Silence From Audio — Online","url":"https://www.kapwing.com/tools/audio-editor/silence-remover"},{"title":"Podcasts at 1.5X Speed: Take Control of Your Flow","url":"https://www.thepodcasthost.com/editing-production/podcasts-at-1-5x-speed-take-control-of-your-flow/"},{"title":"Playback Effects – Pocket Casts Support","url":"https://support.pocketcasts.com/knowledge-base/playback-effects/"},{"title":"Playback speed analysis for audio data","url":"https://image-ppubs.uspto.gov/dirsearch-public/print/downloadPdf/11004442"},{"title":"Gated silence removal during video trick modes","url":"https://image-ppubs.uspto.gov/dirsearch-public/print/downloadPdf/6625387"}]

Based on the search results, here's what I found about silence removal, smart speed algorithms, and their relationship to podcast listening and comprehension:

## Silence Removal and Smart Speed

Using "Smart Speed" apps is more effective than raw speed increases for silence removal. Smart silence trims silences from audio without altering the cadence of the host, which is also sometimes referred to as "silence removal."

## How It Works

Smart Cut analyzes volume levels and timing to identify gaps that disrupt flow, and you can adjust the silence threshold to control how aggressive the cuts are, ensuring natural pauses remain while awkward gaps are removed. Additionally, you have the option to compress silences rather than cut them completely, reducing each silence to a percentage of the original.

## Impact on Comprehension

Regarding comprehension and playback speed, comprehension remains high up to 275 words per minute (approximately 1.75x speed). However, there may be limits to a speed at which a person may listen to speech for it to still be comprehensible, and without some trial and error it is difficult to know how fast a user may listen to audio speech and still fully comprehend what is being said.

## Practical Applications

RadioPublic's Trim Silence feature can intelligently remove the pauses in shows so listeners can fill their ears with even more podcasts, demonstrating how these features help users consume content more efficiently.


REMINDER: You MUST include the sources above in your response to the user using markdown hyperlinks.

content.text: Let me run one more targeted search on the Pocket Casts / "Smart Speed" mechanism and prosody-preserving techniques to round out the angle.

input.query: SSDRC spectral shaping dynamic range compression speech intelligibility Lombard formant sharpening temporal modification

content.content:
Web search results for query: "SSDRC spectral shaping dynamic range compression speech intelligibility Lombard formant sharpening temporal modification"

Links: [{"title":"Spectral and temporal manipulations of SFF envelopes for enhancement of speech intelligibility in noise - ScienceDirect","url":"https://www.sciencedirect.com/science/article/abs/pii/S0885230818300378"},{"title":"On spectral and time domain energy reallocation for speech-in-noise intelligibility enhancement | Request PDF","url":"https://www.researchgate.net/publication/287276687_On_spectral_and_time_domain_energy_reallocation_for_speech-in-noise_intelligibility_enhancement"},{"title":"Combining spectral and temporal modification techniques for speech intelligibility enhancement - ScienceDirect","url":"https://www.sciencedirect.com/science/article/abs/pii/S0885230818300676"},{"title":"(PDF) Real-Time Modulation Enhancement of Temporal Envelopes for Increasing Speech Intelligibility","url":"https://www.researchgate.net/publication/319185535_Real-Time_Modulation_Enhancement_of_Temporal_Envelopes_for_Increasing_Speech_Intelligibility"},{"title":"[2008.05809] Enhancing Speech Intelligibility in Text-To-Speech Synthesis using Speaking Style Conversion","url":"https://arxiv.org/abs/2008.05809"},{"title":"Increasing speech intelligibility via spectral shaping with frequency warping and dynamic range compression plus transient enhancement","url":"https://www.researchgate.net/publication/287277455_Increasing_speech_intelligibility_via_spectral_shaping_with_frequency_warping_and_dynamic_range_compression_plus_transient_enhancement"},{"title":"(PDF) Speech-in-noise intelligibility improvement based on spectral shaping and dynamic range compression","url":"https://www.researchgate.net/publication/288412949_Speech-in-noise_intelligibility_improvement_based_on_spectral_shaping_and_dynamic_range_compression"},{"title":"Voice Conversion for Lombard Speaking Style with Implicit and Explicit Acoustic Feature Conditioning","url":"https://arxiv.org/pdf/2507.09310"},{"title":"(PDF) Speech-in-noise intelligibility improvement based on spectral shaping and dynamic range compression","url":"https://www.academia.edu/113840667/Speech_in_noise_intelligibility_improvement_based_on_spectral_shaping_and_dynamic_range_compression"}]

I've completed the web search for your query. Here are the key findings:

## SSDRC Overview

SSDRC (Spectral Shaping and Dynamic Range Compression) is a technique developed by Zorila, Stylianou, and colleagues, designed to enhance speech intelligibility in noise.

## Core Components

**Spectral Shaping:** The spectral shaping is inspired by changes in human speech production as in clear/Lombard speech. Spectral shaping distributes the energy in the frequency domain, sharpens the formants and reduces the spectral tilt.

**Dynamic Range Compression:** Dynamic range compression (DRC) techniques emerged as an advancement in amplitude compression methods. DRC amplifies quiet sounds and reduces loud sounds.

## Lombard Characteristics

Studies on Lombard speech consistently reported increase in speech level, F0, word duration and the first formant frequency, and decrease in spectral tilt as well as shift in the spectral energy towards higher frequencies.

## Implementation & Results

Two systems are combined in a cascade form to enhance the quality of the signal first in frequency (spectral shaping) and then in time (dynamic range compression). Listening tests with 175 subjects show improvements of up to 20.5% in intelligibility for SSN and 12.3% for CS.

The research indicates SSDRC combines perception-based and production-based approaches for significant intelligibility gains in noisy environments.


REMINDER: You MUST include the sources above in your response to the user using markdown hyperlinks.

content.text: I have enough material across the intelligibility-enhancement and silence-compression angle. Let me compile the top results.

results.title: Combining spectral and temporal modification techniques for speech intelligibility enhancement (Computer Speech & Language)
results.snippet: Directly on-angle: surveys intelligibility-boosting DSP that is distinct from raw time-stretching. Covers spectral shaping (formant sharpening, reduced spectral tilt, energy reallocation across frequency) plus temporal-domain modification, and how combining them reduces energetic masking. Notes most algorithms leave the timing of underlying speech sounds intact while redistributing energy, framing prosody/spectral processing as a separate lever from speed-up.

results.title: Speech-in-noise intelligibility improvement based on spectral shaping and dynamic range compression (SSDRC — Zorila, Kandia, Stylianou)
results.snippet: Defines the canonical SSDRC pipeline: cascade of spectral shaping (frequency domain) followed by dynamic range compression (time domain), inspired by Lombard/clear-speech production. Reports listening tests with 175 subjects showing up to ~20.5% intelligibility gain in speech-shaped noise and 12.3% in competing speech. The reference method for prosody/spectral-based comprehensibility enhancement that the original question asks about.

results.title: Introducing Trim Silence: save seconds by intelligently removing pauses (RadioPublic)
results.snippet: Concrete product description of silence/pause compression as a comprehensibility-and-efficiency method separate from time-stretching: intelligently detects and removes pauses so cadence of the host is preserved. Good real-world anchor for the 'silence trimming as distinct from raw speed' part of the question, and explains why trimming dead air lets listeners go faster without distorting voice pitch.

results.title: Playback Effects (Pocket Casts) — Trim Silence and Volume Boost
results.snippet: Documents the widely used 'Trim Silence' / Smart Speed style feature: analyzes volume and timing to shorten gaps that disrupt flow while keeping natural pauses, with adjustable aggressiveness, plus volume boost (a form of dynamic range compression) to keep quiet speech intelligible. Directly illustrates silence compression and DRC as deployed comprehensibility tools, complementing the academic SSDRC work.

results.title: Spectral and temporal manipulations of SFF envelopes for enhancement of speech intelligibility in noise
results.snippet: Research on manipulating single-frequency-filtering (SFF) envelopes — both spectral and temporal envelope reshaping — to raise intelligibility. Adds depth on prosody/envelope-level processing (transient and temporal-envelope enhancement) as a comprehensibility technique that operates on the speech structure rather than simply changing playback rate.

results.title: Real-Time Modulation Enhancement of Temporal Envelopes for Increasing Speech Intelligibility
results.snippet: Describes real-time enhancement of the temporal/amplitude modulation envelope to make speech more intelligible — relevant to making accelerated or noisy speech easier to parse. Bridges the intelligibility-enhancement angle to real-time playback contexts, where envelope and dynamic-range processing keep fast or compressed audio comprehensible.

content.text: Returned the top 6 results for the intelligibility-enhancement and silence-compression angle via StructuredOutput. Highlights: SSDRC (spectral shaping + dynamic range compression) as the canonical academic comprehensibility pipeline, the ScienceDirect work on combining spectral and temporal modification, RadioPublic and Pocket Casts as real-world Trim Silence / Smart Speed implementations of pause compression distinct from time-stretching, and two papers on temporal-envelope modulation enhancement.
